tasks.title: Cancellation and finalized-state contract

tasks.title: Runtime diagnostics and trace wire stabilization
tasks.definition_of_done: Phase 7 runtime-observability handoff items are closed before the Phase 8 wire contract is documented: startup diagnostics and adapter degradation use structured redacted diagnostics at JSON, RPC, and prompt boundaries; public diagnostic formatting cannot leak real-format provider keys, credentialed URLs, or absolute paths through Diagnostic.message or equivalent fields; RPC trace is reachable through the production RPC path and is scoped per run or explicitly documented; session-recovery and compaction diagnostics flow into the in-process DiagnosticSink; trace and diagnostic tests assert hook blocking, tool validation, cancellation, compaction failure, invalid runtime state, unsupported command, and real-format redaction behavior.

inference_notes.reason: Derived from the registered Phase 8 design's diagnostics/trace success criterion plus Phase 7 handoff residuals that must be closed before stabilizing runtime wire behavior.

No stable 1.0 API promise, TypeScript extension API compatibility, package ecosystem expansion, new adapter kind, web UI product work, provider OAuth, in-core plan mode/sub-agent/todo/permission-popup/MCP runtime, shared opi-types crate, or whole-agent-loop rewrite unless a contract test proves it necessary.

Carries forward Phase 7 residuals: StartupDiagnostics and adapter degradation remained string-shaped, RPC trace production reachability and per-run scope were unresolved, Diagnostic.message could leak sensitive values, and session-recovery/compaction diagnostics were not fully wired to the in-process sink.
Runs after SDK/RPC command-state contracts so wire diagnostics and trace behavior can be stabilized against the production command boundary before final docs and guards.

session_notes.summary: Phase 8 task 8.6: 2 minimal production fixes (G3 harness.rs resume_session_id->record_harness_diagnostic wires session-recovery into the in-process DiagnosticSink matching compaction; G4 rpc.rs response_error_with_code adds 5 stable error_code values on the additive SdkResponse::error_code field, no schema bump) + 13 characterization tests across 8 test files + 1 rpc.rs const-pin unit test + CHANGELOG. 6 of 8 gates were characterization (Phase 7 audit findings already closed by prior hardening commits, now pinned). Independent D.2 evaluator PASS (4 lenses, 0 confirmed blockers, 37 corroborating notes); proactively closed the evaluator's flagged non-blocking residual (2 unasserted error_code wire values) with a const-pin unit test -> 5/5 codes pinned. All mechanical gates green.

Independent D.2 evaluator returned PASS-WITH-CONCERNS with 3 medium guard-quality blockers; all fixed (binding map binds surface->tier per line; exact pub use re-export pins replace bare-substring collision-prone checks; removed broad negation tokens 没有/不添加/不引入 and rephrased ZH docs to narrow 不得/不声明/不会 tokens with documented substring-co-occurrence limitation; strengthened meta-guard) and independently re-verified RESOLVED via mutation testing. All mechanical gates green (2005/0; fmt/clippy/doc/smoke clean).

1.evaluator_summary: All 18 Phase 1 tasks passing. 213 tests green. Audit fixes committed (a66465a): SSE malformed event surfacing, CRLF parsing, BashTool cross-platform, interactive TUI wiring, auth error variant, config base_url/precedence, responsive cancellation. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke.
audit_notes: Audit note (2026-05-22): tasks 1.14, 1.15, 1.16 have evaluator_required=true in ledger but Opi-Evaluator: not-required in commit footers. Root cause: evaluator gate was run but determined these tasks did not require independent evaluation beyond mechanical gates (cli-runtime tier auto-flag is conservative). Commits are not amended; footer is authoritative for audit. Ledger evidence.opi_evaluator now mirrors the actual footer value.

2.evaluator_summary: All 16 Phase 2 tasks passing. 537 tests green. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc.

3.evaluator_summary: All 13 Phase 3 tasks passing. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc. Enterprise providers (Bedrock, Azure, Vertex) reviewed by code-reviewer with bugs fixed before commit.

task_summary.title: pi-style tool selection and safety hooks

audit_notes: Phase 3 hardening (2026-05-28): Cross-audit verification of Codex/GPT-5.5/GLM-5.1 findings.
Fixed: Bedrock retry-after parsing, Azure endpoint validation, Bedrock URL image rejection, ls truncation count, compaction image placeholders, global context files, proxy wiring.
Added: --list-models CLI flag (table + NDJSON), --image non-interactive runtime, prompt_with_content agent method.
Deferred to Phase 4: Picker TUI integration, interactive --image with TUI preview.
Documented scope limitations: Bedrock full ambient credential chain (IMDS/SSO/credential_process), Vertex service-account/ADC.

4.evaluator_summary: All 19 Phase 4 tasks passing. 53 web-ui tests + 9 RPC integration tests + existing test suites all green. Cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke.

5.evaluator_summary: All 9 Phase 5 tasks passing. 1770 workspace tests. 15 documentation guard tests enforce truthful Phase 5 MVP descriptions. Package CLI (add/remove/list/doctor), manifest V2 with adapter declarations, process-JSONL adapter hosting via opi-extension-jsonl-v1, adapter-to-runtime bridging, and runnable example adapter packages all verified. audit_notes: Task 8.7 (final Phase 8 task): the independent D.2 evaluator initially returned PASS-WITH-CONCERNS with 3 confirmed medium guard-quality blockers in the new runtime_contract_docs.rs (tier not bound to surface; lib.rs cross-check bare-substring collision with AgentError/ToolDef; broad Chinese negation tokens 没有/不添加/不引入 co-occurrence leaks). All 3 were fixed before commit (per-line surface->tier binding map; exact `pub use` re-export line pins; removed the broad tokens and rephrased ZH docs to narrow 不得/不声明/不会 tokens with the substring-co-occurrence limitation documented) and independently re-verified RESOLVED via mutation testing.

Residual (non-blocking, documented): the no_positive_claim substring helper shares a per-line co-occurrence limitation with the Phase 6/7 guards (a deceptive line placing a negation word next to a positive claim can leak). Fully closing it requires structural clause parsing across all three phase guards and is out of Phase 8 scope; the Phase 8 helper was kept at the narrow Phase 7 baseline with the limitation documented in its doc-comment.
